mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 70% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint increases in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days exclusively during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases produce negative or flat 20-day forward returns during recovery phases, or the signal direction reverses in expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces tighter monetary policy expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases correlate with positive 20-day forward returns in over 60% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll additions exceeding 150,000 signal robust labor demand and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 150.0 thousand in a single month
failure_condition: Payroll surges above 150,000 are followed by negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases, indicating overheating or policy tightening fears.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP combined with a declining federal funds rate creates a favorable growth-and-liquidity environment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising GDPC1 and falling FEDFUNDS yields negative 60-day forward returns in over 65% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden drop in the unemployment rate during a recessionary trough signals rapid labor market stabilization, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE declines during troughs are followed by negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the signal shows no predictive power outside trough regimes.
